vitals.location: Royal Town of Pekan, Pahang, Malaysia

climate.description: Tropical monsoon climate with warm, humid conditions ideal for road cycling.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 63. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 60.

course_description.character: Flat river-hugging out-and-back with a central loop deviation, featuring moderate rolling hills and short steep pitches amid tropical scenery.
course_description.signature_challenge: Pahang River double-sided routing: 108km flat borders punctuated by a hilly loop for 2500ft gain.
suffering_zones: River Border Push (First 40km): Sustained flat effort along Pahang River banks tests pacing in humid conditions before the loop.
Loop Deviation: Short interior loop introduces the event's 2500ft elevation via rolling short steep hills.
Return River Grind (Last 68km): Fatigue sets in on the flat return, where heat and wind amplify the distance.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotels and homestays in Pekan town; book early for June event weekend.
logistics.transport: UCI Gran Fondo weekend event with 80km/120km distances; international field; held in Royal Town of Pekan.

history.origin_story: UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier attracting international pelotons to Malaysia's Pahang region.

final_verdict.one_liner: Scenic, affordable UCI gran fondo blending flat speed with moderate Pahang hills.
